titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Camille Doucet Correspondence
titlestmt.author: Ève Bourbeau-Allard

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 25, 2019 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection of circa 11,000 letters addressed to arts administrator Camille Doucet (1812-1895) from upwards of 1150 correspondents, offering rich documentation of the French arts and letters in the second half of the 19th century. Many were sent to Doucet in his capacity as head of the administration of theaters in the imperial government of Napoleon III. Other letters discuss the business of the Académie française, as well as patronage, cultural productions, and social events. Correspondents include members of the Académie française, members of the Bonaparte family, writers, actors, theater directors, composers, painters, aristocrats, politicians, military leaders, scientists, and patrons of the arts.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection of circa 11,000 letters addressed to Camille Doucet (1812-1895) from upwards of 1150 correspondents, offering rich documentation of the French arts and letters in the second half of the 19th century. Many were sent to Doucet in his capacity as head of the administration of theaters in the imperial government of Napoleon III between 1853 and 1870. Other letters discuss the business of the Académie française, as well as patronage, cultural productions, and social events. Correspondents include members of the Académie française, members of the Bonaparte family, writers, actors, theater directors, composers, painters, aristocrats, politicians, military leaders, scientists, and patrons of the arts.

There is voluminous correspondence, bound in albums, from Princesse Mathilde Bonaparte, Comédie-Française director Jules Claretie, François Coppée, Alexandre Dumas père and fils, Ludovic Halévy, Arsène and Henry Houssaye, Ernest Legouvé, and Victorien Sardou. Other individuals represented in the collection include George Sand, Émile Zola, Charles Gounod, Jules Massenet, and Henri d'Orléans, duc d'Aumale. Enclosed in a small number of letters are clippings and other printed material, manuscript poems, and manuscript music scores, including by Massenet.

Also included are a small number of outgoing letters by Camille Doucet, letters addressed to Doucet's widow Emma Adelon, and letters to his daughter Antoinette Doucet and her husband, politician René Joseph Brice (1839-1921).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Names of correspondents and dates of creation were transcribed from an inventory supplied with the collection, and have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Names, including titles of nobility and nicknames, have been minimally edited for consistency, but may not always match authorized forms of the name. Correspondence files were oreviously numbered and listed in loose alphabetical order, and have been re-arranged. A copy of the original inventory, which also includes a chronology of Doucet's career, is available in Box 41 for reference.
A letter from G. de Wailly dated 1835 May 25 is listed in the original inventory, but could not be found during processing.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the collection of Camille Doucet, kept by his descendants. Purchased from Christie's on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Camille Doucet Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Camille Doucet Correspondence. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Camille Doucet (1812-1895) was a French dramatist, government official, and member of the Académie française. From 1853 to 1870, during the Second Empire of Napoleon III, Doucet served as the director of the Superintendance générale des théâtres, an administrative unit that oversaw the inspection of theater facilities, the nomination of theater directors, the management of music conservatories, the censorship of new works, and the allocation of state financial sponsorhip. Doucet was named Chevalier de la Légion d'honneur in 1857, and was elected to the Académie française in 1865. In addition to serving on the Commission du dictionnaire, Doucet became the Académie's permanent secretary in 1876.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence Albums, 1852-1906. II. Correspondence Files, 1832-1920.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fourteen albums, bound in the late 19th or early 20th century with bookplates from the library of Camille Doucet, containing letters from ten individuals with particularly voluminous correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters from Napoleon III, Empress Eugénie, and other members of the Bonaparte family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Dumas with actress Adah Isaacs Menken and with an unidentified woman, an agreement between Dumas and Auguste Maquet, and third-party correspondence and documents relating to the inauguration of a monument to the memory of Dumas, dated 1883.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a signed photographic portrait, and printed pamphlets.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Files named after the main correspondent may also include letters written by a spouse or relative, which has not been noted in the inventory in all cases.

did.unittitle: Baroche, Pierre Jules, Alphonse, and Ernest
Beauchesne, Alcide Hyacinthe du Bois de

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters from Paul Beauvallet, El. Beauvallet, and Léon Beauvallet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters from Eleanor Locanie Beauvoir, A.R. de Beauvoir, and G. R. de Beauvoir.

did.unittitle: Blowitz, Henri Georges Stephane Adolphe Opper de
Boisoillon, marquis de, secrétaire du Duc d'Orléans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by printed poems and two broadsides "Élections législatives du 21 aout 1881 - Épitre du Dr. Comte de Boudrant (Horace II), candidat radical legitimiste universel".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes correspondence from Emmanuel de Broglie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photographic portrait of Croizette.

did.unittitle: Delavigne, Casimir and Louis A. C.
Double, Marie, baronne (alias Marie de Peyronny, Etincelle)
Elisabeth, reine de Roumanie (alias Carmen Sylva)
Germiny, Charles and Eugène, comtes de
Gréville, Henry (Alice Marie Céleste Fleury Durand)
Gyp (Sibylle de Mirabeau, comtesse de Martel)
Haussonville, Joseph Othenin Bernard de Cléron, comte d'
Haussonville, Louise de Broglie, comtesse d'
Haussonville, Paul-Gabriel Othenin de Cléron, comte d'
La Pommeraye, Pierre Henri Victor Berdalle de
La Tour D'Auvergne, Edouard, prince de
Luynes, Honoré Théodore Paul Joseph d'Albert, duc de
Massa, Alexandre Philippe Régnier, marquis de
Montalivet, Marthe Camille Bachasson, comte de
Moskowa, general Ney, prince de la
Mouchy, Antoine Just Léon Marie de Noailles, duc de
Nogent Saint Laurens, Edme Jean Joseph Jules Henri
Pedro II de Alcantara, Emperor of Brazil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A photographic portrait is enclosed in the letter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters from Émile Bergerat, Francois Coppée, L. Perrin, and H. E. Perrin.

did.unittitle: Primoli, Joseph, comte and Charlotte Bonaparte Primoli, comtesse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes letters dated 1982-1983 from Sylvie Chevalley to Mme Jacques Dupin regarding Rachel's correspondence and life.

did.unittitle: Sandeau, Jules, Paule, and Marie Simon
Tarente, Alex Charles Macdonald, duc de
Viel-Castel, Horace, comte de, and Louis de Salviac, baron de
Walewski, Alexandre Florian Joseph Colonna, comte, and Alexandra Walewska
